title: Add go tip to build, but allow it to have failures
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Add tip back in and teach travis ci to ignore any failure while using it as that's golang's unstable and under development version.

comments.body:
What a per-snickety piece of syntax that's not well documented.  @rhatdan @nalind this is ready to go.  

Open up the checks and then the Details link for Travis.  You'll see a Red X for "Go: Tip", green the rest of the way and the overall tests pass as "Go: Tip" is in the "Allowed Failures" section.  If we're still Red in a week or so, we can try to figure out the issues there.

If you'd like, I can change the other projects to do the same thing.
Tip gofmt is breaking in libpod and crio also.  Nice maybe we should do this for all of the repos.

@rhatdan The only downside to doing this here or elsewhere is you don't get the Go: tip failure blaring in your eyeballs.  You've got to click down a level or two to see the failure.  The upside is we won't have build failures as frequently and we wont' get them due to some underdevelopment go funkiness.  Personally I think this is the way to go, but up to you, @nalind and others.

LGTM
I will merge since this only adds information.
